sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: SV Werder Bremen, sitting at 8th in the league with 47 points, host FC St. Pauli, who are struggling at 14th with 31 points, in Round 31 at the Weserstadion in Bremen. This matchup promises to be intriguing as Werder Bremen look to solidify their mid-table position while St. Pauli fight for crucial points to climb away from danger. The referee, Tobias Reichel, averages 3.7 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of the encounter. With a history of competitive clashes, both teams will be eager to make their mark in this Bundesliga fixture.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, the results are closely contested with 1 win each for both teams and 2 draws. Their most recent encounter on December 14, 2024, saw Werder Bremen triumph 2-0 away at St. Pauli, with goalkeeper Michael Zetterer earning an impressive 8.2 Sofascore rating as the player of the match. Head-to-head trends show Werder Bremen are unbeaten in the last 3 meetings, which could give them a psychological edge heading into this game.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Werder Bremen come into this match with a strong recent record, having secured 4 wins in their general streak, though that run has not continued. On the other hand, FC St. Pauli are on a positive note with no losses in their last 3 games, a streak that remains active. St. Pauli’s games often feature low-scoring affairs, with 8 of their last 10 matches having less than 2.5 goals. Werder Bremen have seen over 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 matches, hinting at a potentially feisty game, while St. Pauli’s matches often stay under 4.5 cards in 6 of their last 8 outings.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Werder Bremen
sections.text: Werder Bremen boast standout performers like Romano Schmid, who ranks 4th in the league for key passes with 62, and has been in fine form with a 7.52 average Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches, scoring 2 goals and providing 1 assist. Marvin Ducksch, a key forward, has contributed 7 goals and 7 assists this season, though he has underperformed his expected goals of 9.30 by -2.30. Jens Stage is another player to watch with 9 goals, overperforming his expected goals of 6.47 by 2.53. Additionally, defender Anthony Jung impresses with a league-ranked 4th ground duels won percentage of 70.9%, providing solidity at the back.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - FC St. Pauli
sections.text: For FC St. Pauli, forward Morgan Guilavogui leads the attack with 5 goals, though slightly underperforming his expected goals of 6.19. Noah Weißhaupt is notable for drawing fouls, ranking 5th in the league with 2.4 fouls per game, potentially disrupting Werder Bremen’s rhythm. Johannes Eggestein adds creativity with 4 assists, while defender Eric Smith brings stability with a solid 7.12 average rating. St. Pauli will need these players to step up if they are to challenge Werder Bremen’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Werder Bremen have scored 50 goals this season, ranking 9th in the league, and excel in headers with 9 goals, placing them 5th. Their ability to create big chances (79, ranked 8th) and convert 40.5% of them (ranked 7th) shows attacking intent, though they’ve missed 47 big chances (10th least). Defensively, they’ve conceded 56 goals (14th worst) but rank highly in tackles (16.28 per game, 3rd) and interceptions (9.28 per game, 3rd). FC St. Pauli struggle offensively, with only 37 goals scored as per their distribution data, and tend to score late, with 21.6% of goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their defense will need to withstand Werder Bremen’s fast breaks, where the home side ranks 4th with 1.28 per game.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Werder Bremen will miss defenders Derrick Köhn, who has 2 goals and 2 assists, and Miloš Veljković, potentially weakening their backline. FC St. Pauli are without key midfielder Jackson Irvine, who has provided 6 assists this season, and defender Karol Mets, who ranks 3rd in the league for aerial duels won at 70.6%. These absences could impact both teams’ structures, with St. Pauli particularly missing Irvine’s creativity in midfield.
